AnalyseVideoFilesHelpText: Extract video information such as resolution, runtime and codec information from files. This requires {appName} to read parts of the file which may cause high disk or network activity during scans.
AnalyticsEnabledHelpText: Send anonymous usage and error information to {appName}'s servers. This includes information on your browser, which {appName} WebUI pages you use, error reporting as well as OS and runtime version. We will use this information to prioritize features and bug fixes.
ApiKeyValidationHealthCheckMessage: Please update your API key to be at least {length} characters long. You can do this via settings or the config file
ApiPriorityLivestreamIdentificationUnitC: Included in Video Details (no extra API request)
ApiPriorityMetadataItemsHelpText: Select which operations should use the YouTube Data API v3 first, before falling back to page scraping or yt-dlp. Only active when 'Use YouTube Data API for Metadata' is enabled.
ApiPriorityVideoListingUnitCost: ~1 unit per 50 videos (1 unit to resolve uploads, plus 1 unit per uploads playlist page)
AppDataLocationHealthCheckMessage: Updating will not be possible to prevent deleting AppData on Update

AutoTaggingRequiredHelpText: This {implementationName} condition must match for the auto tagging rule to apply. Otherwise a single {implementationName} match is sufficient.

ChannelFootNote: Optionally control truncation to a maximum number of bytes including ellipsis (`...`). Truncating from the end (e.g. `{Channel Title:30}`) or the beginning (e.g. `{Channel Title:-30}`) are both supported.
ChannelTitleToExcludeHelpText: The name of the channel to exclude
ChannelTypesHelpText: Channel type is used for renaming, parsing and searching

ChmodFolderHelpTextWarning: This only works if the user running {appName} is the owner of the file. It's better to ensure the download client sets the permissions properly.
ChownGroupHelpText: Group name or gid. Use gid for remote file systems.
ChownGroupHelpTextWarning: This only works if the user running {appName} is the owner of the file. It's better to ensure the download client uses the same group as {appName}.

ConditionUsingRegularExpressions: This condition matches using Regular Expressions. Note that the characters `\^$.|?*+()[{` have special meanings and need escaping with a `\`
ConnectSettingsSummary: Notifications, connections to media servers/players, and custom scripts
ConnectionLostReconnect: {appName} will try to connect automatically, or you can click reload below.

ConnectionSettingsUrlBaseHelpText: Adds a prefix to the {connectionName} url, such as {url}
CopyUsingHardlinksChannelHelpText: Hardlinks allow {appName} to import existing files to the channel folder without taking extra disk space or copying the entire contents of the file. Hardlinks will only work if the source and destination are on the same volume
CopyUsingHardlinksHelpTextWarning: Occasionally, file locks may prevent renaming files that are in use. You may temporarily stop any active processes using those files and use {appName}'s rename function as a work around.
CreateEmptyChannelFoldersHelpText: Create missing channel folders during disk scan
CuratedPlaylistOrderHelp: Drag to set order for native YouTube playlists and custom playlists together. Top = lowest priority number (0, 1, 2, …). Custom rows are tagged. Tie-break: same priority number lists native playlists before custom; within native playlists, order follows multi-match tie-breakers / activity.
CustomColonReplacementFormatHelpText: Characters to be used as a replacement for colons
CustomColonReplacementFormatHint: Valid file system character such as Colon (Letter)
CustomFormatHelpText: {appName} scores each release using the sum of scores for matching custom formats. If a new release would improve the score, at the same or better quality, then {appName} will grab it.
CustomFormatsSettingsTriggerInfo: A Custom Format will be applied to a release or file when it matches at least one of each of the different condition types chosen.
CustomFormatsSpecificationExceptLanguage: Matches if any language other than the selected language is present
CustomFormatsSpecificationMaximumSizeHel: Release must be less than or equal to this size
CustomFormatsSpecificationMinimumSizeHel: Release must be greater than this size
CustomFormatsSpecificationRegularExpress: Custom Format RegEx is Case Insensitive
CustomPlaylistHelpInNotIn: For in/notIn use a JSON array (e.g. ["PLid1","PLid2"]) or comma-separated values.
CustomPlaylistsHelp: Filter-based playlists (max 5 rules each). Drag order vs native playlists is set in Playlist priority above. With custom NFOs and playlist folders, each custom playlist uses its own folder Season 10001, Season 10002, … so it never overlaps native curated playlists (Season 02, Season 03, …).
DailyVideoTypeDescription: Date-driven or frequent uploads where the calendar day is the main identifier
DefaultDelayProfileChannel: This is the default profile. It applies to all channels that don't have an explicit profile.

DownloadClientPriorityHelpText: Download Client Priority from 1 (Highest) to 50 (Lowest). Default: 1. Round-Robin is used for clients with the same priority.
DownloadClientRemovesCompletedDownloadsH: Download client {downloadClientName} is set to remove completed downloads. This can result in downloads being removed from your client before {appName} can import them.
DownloadClientRootFolderHealthCheckMessa: Download client {downloadClientName} places downloads in the root folder {rootFolderPath}. You should not download to a root folder.
DownloadClientSettingsCategoryHelpText: Adding a category specific to {appName} avoids conflicts with unrelated non-{appName} downloads. Using a category is optional, but strongly recommended.
DownloadClientSettingsCategorySubFolderH: Adding a category specific to {appName} avoids conflicts with unrelated non-{appName} downloads. Using a category is optional, but strongly recommended. Creates a [category] subdirectory in the output directory.
DownloadClientSettingsDestinationHelpTex: Manually specifies download destination, leave blank to use the default
DownloadClientSettingsInitialStateHelpTe: Initial state for downloads added to {clientName}
DownloadClientSettingsOlderPriorityVideo: Priority to use when grabbing videos that aired over 14 days ago
DownloadClientSettingsPostImportCategory: Category for {appName} to set after it has imported the download. Leave blank to keep same category.
DownloadClientSettingsRecentPriorityVide: Priority to use when grabbing videos that aired within the last 14 days
DownloadClientSettingsUrlBaseHelpText: Adds a prefix to the {clientName} url, such as {url}
DownloadClientSettingsUseSslHelpText: Use secure connection when connection to {clientName}

DownloadLibraryThumbnailsHelpText: Write episode thumbnail JPEGs next to completed downloads (and refresh them from the scheduled “Download new thumbnails” task). Independent of custom NFOs. Thumbnails are also written while the Plex metadata provider is enabled (local art for Plex). Turning the Plex provider on sets this checkbox on automatically; clearing it still leaves thumbnails on if Plex stays enabled.

GetVideoDetailsTooltip: Run the hydration step (same as Refresh & Scan): fill missing video detail metadata for this channel

Label: Label
LibraryImportChannelHeader: Add channels you already have on disk to TubeArr
LibraryImportScanUnmonitoredFoldersBlurb: Use the search icon on a root folder row to scan for immediate subfolders that are not already a channel library path in TubeArr. TubeArr uses the same resolve path as Library Import (folder name and sample files) to suggest channels. Then click the path to open Library Import and add those channels.
LibraryImportSelectRowsToAdd: When TubeArr matches a folder to a channel, that row is checked for import automatically. Change the checkboxes if you want, then press Import at the bottom. Rows still searching need you to pick a channel first. Channels already in TubeArr stay disabled here.
LibraryImportTips: Some tips to ensure the import goes smoothly:
LibraryImportTipsChannelUseRootFolder: Point TubeArr at the library root: one folder that holds a subfolder per YouTube channel—not a single channel’s folder. Good: "`{goodFolderExample}`". Bad: "`{badFolderExample}`" (that path is one channel; use its parent as the root instead).
LibraryImportTipsDontUseDownloadsFolder: Do not use Library Import for your download client’s output. TubeArr expects an already organized library layout, not a pile of unsorted files.
LibraryImportTipsQualityInVideoFilename: Include quality (and season/episode where relevant) in file names so TubeArr can parse and rename reliably, e.g. `video.s02e15.bluray.mkv`.

MaximumSizeHelpText: Maximum size for a release to be grabbed in MB. Set to zero to set to unlimited
MediaInfoFootNote:
Media Info tokens are sourced from yt-dlp metadata:

- **Codec**: `vcodec`
- **Audio Codec**: `acodec`
- **Resolution**: `height`
- **Framerate**: `fps`
- **HDR/SDR**: `dynamic_range`
- **Audio Channels**: `audio_channels`
- **Bitrate**: `tbr` (total) or `abr` (audio-only)
- **Container**: `ext`

PlaylistMultiMatchStrategyDragHelp: Drag to order tie-breakers when a video belongs to more than one curated playlist. The top rule is applied first after YouTube playlist priority; lower rules break ties.
PlaylistMultiMatchStrategyHelpText: When a video is in multiple curated playlists, TubeArr picks one playlist for the file path and metadata. YouTube playlist priority is compared first; this list orders how remaining ties are broken.
PlaylistPassTruncated: Only latest 25 playlists are shown, go to details to see all playlists
PlexProviderBasePathHelpText: If set, the provider is also served at /{basePath}/tv (in addition to /tv). Example: plex
PlexProviderExposeArtworkUrlsHelpText: When enabled, episode/season/show payloads include remote thumbnail URLs (YouTube CDN and channel/playlist art). When disabled, only a local {basename}-thumb.jpg next to the video is exposed via TubeArr (if present); Plex may otherwise generate its own stills. Timeline scrub preview thumbnails (BIF) are a separate Plex Media Server library setting (Settings → Library → Video preview thumbnails).
PlexProviderIncludeChildrenByDefaultHelp: When enabled, show/season metadata responses include their children (seasons/episodes) unless Plex requests otherwise.

RefreshAndScanTooltip: Fetch playlists and videos from YouTube via yt-dlp and update the channel. After hydration, runs a livestream-identification pass (longform videos first, then Shorts). Requires yt-dlp configured in Settings -> Tools -> yt-dlp.

UrlBaseHelpText: For reverse proxy support, default is empty
UseCustomNfosHelpText: Write Kodi/Plex-style NFO files (tvshow, season, episode) next to media when a download completes. When enabled, channels that use playlist folders store each native YouTube playlist under Season 02, Season 03, … and each custom (rule-based) playlist under Season 10001, Season 10002, … so the two never share a folder. Settings → Naming → playlist folder format is used only when this is off. Disable if you manage metadata another way.
UseYouTubeApi: Use YouTube Data API for Metadata
UseYouTubeApiHelpText: When enabled, the YouTube Data API v3 will be used as a priority or fallback source for the operations selected below. Requires a valid API key.
VideoGrabbedTooltip: Video grabbed from {indexer} and sent to {downloadClient}
VideoImportedTooltip: Video downloaded successfully and picked up from download client
VideoMissingAbsoluteNumber: Video does not have an absolute video number
VideoSearchResultsLoadError: Unable to load results for this video search. Try again later
VideoTitleFootNote: Optionally control truncation to a maximum number of bytes including ellipsis (`...`). Truncating from the end (e.g. `{Video Title:30}`) or the beginning (e.g. `{Video Title:-30}`) are both supported. Video titles will be automatically truncated to file system limitations if necessary.
VideoTitleRequiredHelpText: Prevent importing for up to 48 hours if the video title is in the naming format and the video title is TBA
VisitTheWikiForMoreDetails: Visit the wiki for more details:
WantMoreControlAddACustomFormat: Want more control over which downloads are preferred? Add a [Custom Format](/settings/customformats)
WeekColumnHeaderHelpText: Shown above each column when week is the active view
WhyCantIFindMyShow: Why can't I find my show?
WouldYouLikeToRestoreBackup: Would you like to restore the backup '{name}'?
YouTubeApiKeyHelpText: Google Cloud API key with YouTube Data API v3 enabled. Used for channel search and adding channels.
YouTubeApiKeyOptionalPerformanceNote: A key is not required. When enabled, metadata operations are typically about 12× faster than relying on yt-dlp alone. Configure yt-dlp under
YouTubeApiKeyPlaceholder: Enter your YouTube Data API v3 key
YouTubeApiSearchResolveRecommendation: Recommendation: turn this off unless you specifically need API-first behavior. yt-dlp resolve/search speed is typically comparable without consuming API quota.
YouTubeApiUnitCostsWarning: YouTube Data API quota is consumed per request. Unit costs for the supported operations are:
YouTubeChannelIdExcludeHelpText: The YouTube channel ID of the channel to exclude
YouTubeQualityAdvancedSectionDescription: Fine-tune quality selection and fallback behavior.
YouTubeQualityAllowMuxedFallbackHelpText: If separate streams are unavailable, allow single muxed format
YouTubeQualityAudioArgsHelp: Audio extraction and conversion args (for example: -x, --audio-format, --audio-quality).
YouTubeQualityFallbackDegradeResolutionH: If the preferred format is unavailable, allow stepping down resolution (and FPS) in configured steps until a format is found, while still respecting min height.
YouTubeQualityFallbackNextBestHelp: Pick the single best available format for the video, even if it exceeds your max or falls below your target. Use when you want to avoid failures and accept any reasonable quality.
YouTubeQualityFallbackNextBestWithinCeil: Pick the best available format that does not exceed your max height/FPS. Prefer formats closest to your target, then relax audio or video codec if needed.
YouTubeQualityFallbackStrictHelp: Never use a format below your preferred quality. If the exact format is unavailable, the download fails.
YouTubeQualityMuxArgsHelp: Extra muxing and transcoding args (for example: --remux-video, --recode-video, --postprocessor-args / --ppa).
YouTubeQualityOutputContainerFfmpegNotCo: Containers marked as requiring FFmpeg are unavailable until FFmpeg is configured in Settings > Tools.
YouTubeQualityPowerUserSectionDescriptio: Optional yt-dlp arguments for advanced workflows.
YouTubeQualityPreferSeparateStreamsHelpT: Downloads the best video and audio streams separately and merges them to produce the highest possible quality (common for 1080p+ videos). If disabled, TubeArr downloads a single combined stream, which may be simpler but often lower quality.
YouTubeQualityTimeArgsHelp: Time and chapter manipulation args (for example: --download-sections, --split-chapters, --remove-chapters, --force-keyframes-at-cuts).
YouTubeSettingsSummary: Configure YouTube Data API key for channel search and metadata.
YtDlpCookieExportBrowserHelpText: TubeArr reads YouTube cookies from this browser's profile. That browser should be running and logged into YouTube when you export.
YtDlpCookiesPathHelpText: Optional path to a Netscape-format cookies file. This can be used to provide authentication cookies (e.g., from your browser) to yt-dlp for accessing restricted content.
YtDlpDownloadPathPlaceholder: Path where the binary will be saved
YtDlpExecutablePathHelpText: Full path to the yt-dlp executable used by TubeArr for metadata retrieval and downloads.
YtDlpSettingsDescription: yt-dlp is used by TubeArr to retrieve metadata and download videos.
YtDlpSimultaneousDownloadsHelpText: How many videos may download at once (separate yt-dlp processes). Allowed range: 1–10.
YtDlpSimultaneousDownloadsWarning: Higher values increase the chance that YouTube rate-limits or blocks your IP. Use 1 unless you understand the risk.
YtDlpUpdateDescription: Run the configured yt-dlp executable with -U to update to the latest version.
